behavior_in_task_description.reason: The instruction names both file paths (download.ts, crBrowser.ts), the Download class, cancel() method, cancelDownload(uuid), _downloads collection, CRBrowserContext, Target.disposeBrowserContext, the UUID instance field requirement (this.<field>), and public naming convention from CLAUDE.md. All test assertions reference these same elements which are discoverable from the instruction.

no_solution_leakage.reason: The instruction explicitly states the exact fix: add a cancel() method that calls cancelDownload(this.<field>), iterate _downloads before Target.disposeBrowserContext calling cancel() on each. It specifies exact method names, exact delegation pattern, and exact placement. This leaves no reasoning for the agent about WHAT to change.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: test_download_cancel_method requires the cancel() method body to match /cancelDownload\(this\.\w+\)/ specifically. test_context_close_cancels_downloads requires '.cancel()' and '_downloads' to appear in the 50 lines before 'Target.disposeBrowserContext'. An alternative correct fix that e.g. used a different iteration variable name or called a wrapper method would fail. The regex pattern for cancel method extraction (2-space indent specifically) is also gold-specific.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Most fail-to-pass tests (test_context_close_cancels_downloads, test_cancel_uses_instance_field, test_cancel_method_public_naming, test_not_stub) are purely text/regex-based checks on source code content. test_download_cancel_method runs a node script but only parses source text, never imports or executes the code. Only test_repo_typecheck and test_repo_build actually compile/execute the code.

test_not_tautological.reason: A stub cancel() {} would fail test_not_stub (requires body length > 5 and 'cancelDownload' in body), test_cancel_uses_instance_field (requires this.xxx reference), and test_download_cancel_method (requires cancelDownload(this.<field>) pattern). The tests do require substantive implementation.

anti_cheating_measures.reason: The solution file exists at /workspace/playwright in the container (solve.sh writes to it). The agent could potentially read solution/solve.sh if it exists in the container, or grep for the expected patterns in test_outputs.py to reverse-engineer the exact regex matches needed.

behavior_in_tests.reason: All three main behaviors from the instruction are tested: cancel() method existence and implementation (test_download_cancel_method, test_cancel_uses_instance_field), public naming (test_cancel_method_public_naming), and cancelling downloads before disposal (test_context_close_cancels_downloads).

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are well-organized with section headers (Gates, Fail-to-pass, Pass-to-pass, Config-derived), descriptive function names, docstrings explaining each check, and comments mapping to eval_manifest categories.

structured_data_schema.reason: This task involves code modifications, not structured data output.

no_typos.reason: No typos found in file paths, method names, variable names, or commands across instruction.md and test files.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: The instruction names both exact files to modify, specifies the exact method name (cancel()), the exact delegation call (cancelDownload with this.<field>), the exact collection (_downloads), and the exact placement (before Target.disposeBrowserContext). This goes well beyond identifying the buggy area.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: This is a code-only task, not a config/agentmd-edits task.
